features.details: Inspect the homepage HTML structure and CSS to identify which containers or elements are causing the excessive right-side blank space on large screens. Document the current max-width values, container classes, and responsive breakpoints.

features.description: Identify optimal width values for large screens
features.details: Determine appropriate max-width values and breakpoints for large screens (e.g., 1440px, 1920px, 2560px). Consider readability and design guidelines for content width on large displays.

features.description: Implement responsive width CSS for large screens
features.details: Add or modify CSS media queries to adjust container widths for large screens. This may include increasing max-width values, using percentage-based widths with max-width constraints, or implementing a fluid layout that adapts to viewport size while maintaining readability.

features.description: Adjust homepage content layout for large screens
features.details: If the homepage uses a grid or flex layout, adjust the number of columns or the distribution of content for large screens. Consider using CSS Grid with auto-fit/auto-fill or adjusting flex-basis values to better utilize available space.

features.description: Test responsive behavior across different screen sizes
features.details: Test the homepage on various screen sizes (1366px, 1440px, 1920px, 2560px) using browser developer tools. Verify that the layout adapts smoothly without excessive white space or content overflow.

features.description: Add visual regression tests for responsive layout
features.details: Create or update visual regression tests to capture screenshots of the homepage at different viewport sizes, ensuring future changes don't reintroduce the white space issue.

overall_approach: Analyze the current homepage layout structure to identify the root cause of the excessive right-side blank space on large screens, then implement responsive CSS changes (likely using max-width constraints, flexbox/grid adjustments, or container width modifications) to ensure the layout adapts properly to larger viewport sizes
potential_risks: Changes may affect layout on medium-sized screens, causing unintended side effects
Existing responsive breakpoints may conflict with new large-screen styles
Content may become too stretched if width increases too much without proper constraints
May need to adjust image sizes or aspect ratios for larger containers
